creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) As workers worry about AI, Nvidia’s Jensen Huang says AI is ‘creating an enormous number of jobs’
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: The Nvidia CEO seems to feel that claims of AI's job-killing potential have been greatly exaggerated.
contentSnippet: The Nvidia CEO seems to feel that claims of AI's job-killing potential have been greatly exaggerated.

creator: Julie Bort
title: (No AI) OpenAI’s cozy partner Cerebras is on track for a blockbuster IPO
dc:creator: Julie Bort
content: AI chip maker Cerebras is heading for a blockbuster IPO that could value it at $26.6 billion or more. Its relationship with OpenAI is deep and rich.
contentSnippet: AI chip maker Cerebras is heading for a blockbuster IPO that could value it at $26.6 billion or more. Its relationship with OpenAI is deep and rich.

title: (No AI) OpenAI’s president does ‘all the things,’ except answer a question
author: Elizabeth Lopatto
content:
![Graphic photo collage of Sam Altman and Elon Musk.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/268474_musk_vs_altman_CVirginia3.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

When the bromance sours, we all end up in court. | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images

The strongest witness for Elon Musk's case against OpenAI so far has been Greg Brockman's journal. Brockman himself is running as a close second.

Brockman was called to the stand in a rather unusual way - he was cross-examined first, followed by a direct examination - and he had some serious high school debate club energy. There was a lot of "I wouldn't characterize it that way," "I wouldn't say it that way," and "That sounds like something I wrote. Can I see it in context?" When Musk's attorney, Steven Molo, read some of the evidence aloud, Brockman would pedantically correct him if he skipped a word, even if that word was "a" or "the." Wh …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/923684/musk-brockman-altman-openai-trial)
contentSnippet:
When the bromance sours, we all end up in court. | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images	

The strongest witness for Elon Musk's case against OpenAI so far has been Greg Brockman's journal. Brockman himself is running as a close second.
Brockman was called to the stand in a rather unusual way - he was cross-examined first, followed by a direct examination - and he had some serious high school debate club energy. There was a lot of "I wouldn't characterize it that way," "I wouldn't say it that way," and "That sounds like something I wrote. Can I see it in context?" When Musk's attorney, Steven Molo, read some of the evidence aloud, Brockman would pedantically correct him if he skipped a word, even if that word was "a" or "the." Wh …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) The creator of Roomba is back with a furry robot companion
author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/Golden.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The first “Familiar” from Colin Angle’s new startup, Familiar Machines & Magic, is a robot that is designed to live in your home and interact autonomously with family members. | Image: Familiar Machines and Magic

Colin Angle, the maker of the Roomba and the man who helped put [50 million household robots](https://www.businessinsider.com/roomba-irobot-bankruptcy-warning-rise-fall-2025-11) into people's homes, is back with a new robot. But this one is designed as a companion, not a cleaner.

The first robot from Angle's new company, [Familiar Machines & Magic](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/roomba-pioneer-colin-angle-unveils-new-venture-familiar-machines--magic-introducing-a-new-platform-for-consumer-physical-ai-302761495.html), is a dog-sized robotic pet that resembles a cross between a bear, a barn owl, and a golden retriever. It has an expressive face, with movable eyebrows, ears, and eyes, and the company calls it a "Familiar," a name meant to evoke folklore around the idea of a supernatural companion. Based on a demo video I saw ahead of its appearance at the [WSJ Future of Everything](https://futureofeverything.wsj.com/) conference this w …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/922947/roomba-creator-new-robot-familiar-machines-magic-ai-launch)

title: (No AI) OpenAI and PwC collaborate to reimagine the office of the CFO
content: OpenAI and PwC are partnering to help enterprises use AI agents to automate finance workflows, improve forecasting, strengthen controls, and modernize the CFO function.
contentSnippet: OpenAI and PwC are partnering to help enterprises use AI agents to automate finance workflows, improve forecasting, strengthen controls, and modernize the CFO function.

creator: Todd Feathers
title: (No AI) He Couldn’t Land a Job Interview. Was AI to Blame?
dc:creator: Todd Feathers
content: Armed with some Python and a white-hot sense of injustice, one medical student spent six months trying to figure out whether an algorithm trashed his job application.
contentSnippet: Armed with some Python and a white-hot sense of injustice, one medical student spent six months trying to figure out whether an algorithm trashed his job application.

creator: Maxwell Zeff, Paresh Dave
title: (No AI) Greg Brockman Defends $30B OpenAI Stake: ‘Blood, Sweat, and Tears’
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff, Paresh Dave
content: OpenAI’s cofounder and president revealed in federal court on Monday that he’s one of the largest individual stakeholders in the AI lab.
contentSnippet: OpenAI’s cofounder and president revealed in federal court on Monday that he’s one of the largest individual stakeholders in the AI lab.
